title: In Vivo T1 Mapping of Blood-Brain Barrier Leakage in Mouse Brain Using MRI
author: Vladimir Grouza

steps.title: Note the body weight of each mouse before induction
steps.instruction: Note the body weight of each mouse before induction.

steps.title: Induce and maintain anesthesia with isoflurane during MRI acquisition
steps.instruction: Induce and maintain anesthesia with isoflurane during MRI acquisition. Anesthesia should be maintained at 2-3% isoflurane.

steps.title: The contrast agent should be diluted in saline before administration
steps.instruction: The contrast agent should be diluted in saline before administration.

steps.title: Repeat the full RARE-VTR acquisition after...
steps.instruction: Repeat the full RARE-VTR acquisition after contrast administration using the same geometry and TR values as the pre-contrast scan.

steps.title: Reconstruct images
steps.instruction: Reconstruct images: Reconstruct the raw MRI data offline using the reconstruction workflow based on Bruker2Nifti.

steps.title: Perform motion correction
steps.instruction: Perform motion correction: For each RARE-VTR dataset, compute a rigid-body transformation between the TR = 8000 ms image and each of the other TR volumes. Apply motion correction to align all volumes within each 4D dataset.

steps.title: Denoise and correct ringing artifact
steps.instruction: Denoise and correct ringing artifact: Apply Marcenko-Pastur principal component analysis denoising and Gibbs-ringing correction using DiPy v1.4.0.

steps.title: Apply bias-field correction
steps.instruction: Apply bias-field correction: Perform bias-field correction on the brain-masked TR = 8000 ms image using the N3 algorithm in Advanced Normalization Tools (ANTs). Apply the estimated field to the remaining volumes in the dataset.

steps.title: Register images to an atlas
steps.instruction: Register images to an atlas: Register the extracted and corrected mouse brain image to a high-resolution anatomical template at 40 × 40 × 40 µm using affine registration with 12 degrees of freedom. Then invert the transformation and project the atlas labels back into the native RARE-VTR space for ROI parcellation. Use nearest-neighbor interpolation for labels.

steps.title: Fit voxel-wise T1 maps
steps.instruction: Fit voxel-wise T1 maps: Fit the monoexponential saturation-recovery model voxel-wise using a nonlinear least-squares solver in MATLAB. Generate whole-brain pre- and post-contrast T1 maps for each specimen.

steps.title: Quantify regional T1 shift
steps.instruction: Quantify regional T1 shift: Within each ROI, generate histograms of T1 values from the pre-contrast and post-contrast maps. Quantify the magnitude of contrast-associated T1 shortening using Earth Mover’s Distance between the paired histograms. Figure 1. Representative coronal RARE-VTR images acquired before and after GBCA injection. The RARE-VTR sequence acquires T1-weighted images at multiple repetition times to sample the saturation recovery curve in each voxel for T1 mapping. Successful administration of the GBCA is indicated by visible post-contrast hyperintensity in soft tissues.

steps.title: Figure 2
steps.instruction: Figure 2. Overview of the image-processing workflow for T1 mapping and regional brain delineation. This figure presents the main steps of the image-processing pipeline used for T1 mapping and region-of-interest (ROI) analysis. The images are shown in three anatomical orientations: coronal (left), axial (middle), and sagittal (right). Panel (i) shows the TR = 8000 ms image after correction of imaging artefacts. Panel (ii) shows the brain mask generated using the SHERM-based segmentation method. Panel (iii) shows the atlas labels transformed back onto the native TR = 8000 ms image using the inverse affine transformation. Each color corresponds to a distinct anatomical ROI used for downstream segmentation and analysis.

steps.title: Figure 3
steps.instruction: Figure 3. T1 value distributions for each ROI obtained using nonlinear least square fitting.

steps.title: %s %s
steps.instruction: %s %s

steps.title: Acquire the full pre-contrast RARE-VTR dataset...
steps.instruction: Acquire the full pre-contrast RARE-VTR dataset using the TR series: 8000 , 3600, 2400, 1480, 940, 650, and 501.1 ms.

steps.title: Use the same geometry for all...
steps.instruction: Use the same geometry for all animals and ensure consistent slice placement across mice.

steps.title: Apply eye gel on the eyes to prevent corneal damage
steps.instruction: Apply eye gel on the eyes to prevent corneal damage.

steps.title: Clean the tail vein with 70%...
steps.instruction: Clean the tail vein with 70% ethanol and insert the catheter in the tail vein. Secure the catheter so that there is no blood loss.

steps.title: Position the mouse securely in the...
steps.instruction: Position the mouse securely in the MRI machine and maintain physiological stability throughout imaging.

steps.title: Body temperature and respiration should be monitored continuously
steps.instruction: Body temperature and respiration should be monitored continuously.

steps.title: Immediately after GBCA administration, perform a...
steps.instruction: Immediately after GBCA administration, perform a rapid QC scan, such as a single RARE acquisition, and confirm visible post-contrast hyperintensity in soft tissue.

steps.title: Inject gadolinium based contract agent (GBCA)...
steps.instruction: Inject gadolinium based contract agent (GBCA) via the tail vein at 0.4 mmol/kg .

steps.title: Additionally inject 500 µL saline intraperitoneally...
steps.instruction: Additionally inject 500 µL saline intraperitoneally to keep the mouse hydrated.

steps.title: Minimize motion and avoid any repositioning...
steps.instruction: Minimize motion and avoid any repositioning between pre- and post-contrast acquisitions.

steps.title: Extract the brain
steps.instruction: Extract the brain: Segment the brain from skull and surrounding tissue using the SHERM algorithm implemented in MATLAB (Version R2019b).
